Clinical Psychology Unit

MENU

The Clinical Psychology Department at the Mecidiyeköy campus provides free public psychological assistance and psychotherapy services. We offer help for a variety of psychological issues, such as depression, anxiety, post-traumatic stress disorder, eating disorders, shyness, and communication disorders. Our services are only available to adults aged 18 and over, and we do not offer assistance to children. We do not handle drug-related evaluations or treatments, life-threatening problems, psychotic disorders, or substance abuse issues. In such cases, we refer clients to experts or institutions with whom we collaborate. Our psychotherapy services are conducted by graduate students in the Clinical Psychology Master's program and consist of eight sessions supervised by faculty members of the Clinical Psychology Psychotherapy Program.

GENERAL INFORMATION

1.

Individuals identified as potential beneficiaries are referred to psychologists specializing in our Program. Based on the evaluation interview, they may be directed to other specialized areas, institutions, or centers related to their psychological concerns if necessary.

2.

A tailored psychotherapy plan is developed for each individual's specific issues during the psychotherapy process. Treatment methods primarily focus on psychodynamic psychotherapy and cognitive behavioral therapy.

3.

Our therapists are psychologists who received BA diplomas in psychology and are currently in the final year of their clinical psychology specialization at Okan University.
